44.reason: The loop condition i<1000000 causes the program to iterate beyond the actual string length, printing uninitialized characters (garbage) after the string ends. The loop should stop at the null terminator.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  The loop condition i<1000000 causes the program to iterate beyond the actual string length, printing uninitialized characters (garbage) after the string ends. The loop should stop at the null terminator.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The loop runs up to 1000000 regardless of actual string length, causing garbage output after the null terminator. Also, when a '-' is encountered and none of the valid expansion conditions are met, it prints nothing instead of printing '-'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The loop runs up to 1000000 regardless of actual string length, causing garbage output after the null terminator. Also, when '-' is encountered and none of the expansion conditions are met, it prints nothing instead of '-', violating the requirement to output unchanged input in such cases.]

7.reason: Loop should stop at the string's null terminator; continues to array end, printing null/extra characters.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  Loop should stop at the string's null terminator; continues to array end, printing null/extra characters.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Loop should iterate until end of string, e.g., i<strlen(st) or st[i]!='\0'; otherwise it reads garbage beyond string]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  The loop iterates up to a fixed large number instead of the actual string length, causing it to print null characters or garbage after the string ends. It should iterate until st[i] == '\0' or use strlen(st).
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  The loop should iterate until the null terminator (st[i] != '\0') instead of a fixed large number, otherwise it will print null characters or garbage after the input string ends.

41.reason: Missing an else clause to print the '-' character when none of the expansion conditions are met. According to requirements, invalid ranges should output the input unchanged.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  Missing an else clause to print the '-' character when none of the expansion conditions are met. According to requirements, invalid ranges should output the input unchanged.

10.reason: When '-' does not meet expansion criteria, it should still be printed. Currently, it prints nothing for invalid cases. An else branch is needed inside this block to output '-'.
